name: Paseo de Poniente de Almerimar — circular from El Ejido
name_original: Paseo de Poniente de Almerimar — circular desde El Ejido
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around El Ejido offer a diverse landscape for cyclists. The region features a mix of coastal paths, natural reserves, and varied elevation. Riders can explore less technical routes through sand dunes and coastal wetlands, or encounter more challenging terrain in the nearby hills. The area provides options ranging from sea-level rides to routes with significant elevation changes. Best touring cycling routes around El Ejido The most popular touring cycling route is Paseo de Poniente de Almerimar — circular from El Ejido, a 20.3 miles (32.7 km) trail that takes 1 hour 59 minutes to complete. This moderate route follows paved surfaces along the seafront. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Almerimar Tower – Guardias Viejas Castle loop from Balerma, a moderate 18.7 miles (30.1 km) path. This route includes coastal sections and passes historical sites. Local touring cyclists also love the Guardias Viejas Castle – Almerimar Tower loop from Bar Restaurante Mar Azul, a 15.5 miles (24.9 km) trail leading through coastal areas and past historical landmarks, often completed in about 1 hour 38 minutes. Touring cycling around El Ejido is defined by coastal paths, natural reserves like Punta Entinas-Sabinar, and varied elevation. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy rides to more challenging routes. The routes in El Ejido are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 230 reviews. More than 1500 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore El Ejido's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around El Ejido?
A: There are over 60 touring cycling routes available around El Ejido. These routes offer a diverse landscape, from coastal paths to more challenging inland terrain, catering to various skill levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in El Ejido?
A: El Ejido offers a diverse mix of terrain for touring cyclists. You can find less technical routes through sand dunes, salt marshes, and coastal wetlands, particularly in areas like the Punta Entinas-Sabinar Natural Reserve. For those seeking more challenging rides, the region also features hilly tracks and significant elevation changes in areas leading towards the Sierra de Gádor mountains.

Q: When is the best time of year for touring cycling in El Ejido?
A: While touring cycling is enjoyable year-round, May and August are noted as popular months for cycling in El Ejido. The region's climate generally allows for pleasant riding conditions for much of the year, but it's always good to check local weather forecasts.

Q: Are there touring cycling routes suitable for beginners in El Ejido?
A: Yes, El Ejido offers several routes suitable for beginners. There are 13 easy touring cycling routes, many of which feature minimal elevation gain and follow paved surfaces, such as coastal paths. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31541347" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guardias Viejas Castle – Almerimar Tower loop from Bar Restaurante Mar Azul&lt;/a&gt;, which is an easy 15.5-mile (24.9 km) trail.

Q: What options are there for experienced touring cyclists seeking a challenge?
A: For experienced touring cyclists, El Ejido provides 18 difficult routes with significant elevation changes. These routes often venture into the more hilly inland areas, offering a rigorous experience. An example of a challenging route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38271977" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of La Rábita Beach – View of La Herradura Beach loop from Estadio Municipal de Santo Domingo&lt;/a&gt;, which covers over 100 km with substantial ascents.

Q: Are there family-friendly touring cycling routes in El Ejido?
A: While specific 'family-friendly' designations aren't always explicit, many of the easy and moderate routes with paved surfaces and minimal elevation are suitable for families. Coastal paths, like those around Almerimar, offer pleasant and safe environments for cycling with children. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40386741" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guardias Viejas Castle – Almerimar Tower loop from Lago Victoria&lt;/a&gt; is an easy option that could be suitable.

Q: Are there circular touring cycling routes in El Ejido?
A: Yes, many touring cycling routes in El Ejido are designed as circular loops, allowing you to start and end at the same point. A popular example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1054896377/paseo-de-poniente-de-almerimar-circular-from-el-ejido" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Paseo de Poniente de Almerimar — circular from El Ejido&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 20.3-mile (32.7 km) trail.

Q: What interesting landmarks or natural features can I see along the touring cycling routes?
A: Along the touring cycling routes in El Ejido, you can explore several interesting landmarks and natural features. Highlights include the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/744872" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guardias Viejas Castle&lt;/a&gt;, the serene &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1501230" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Entrance to Punta Entinas-Sabinar Natural Park&lt;/a&gt; with its unique coastal ecosystems, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1383204" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salinas and Wetlands of Roquetas de Mar&lt;/a&gt;, which are important for birdlife. You might also pass by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1401503" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Faro Beach and Roquetas de Mar Lighthouse&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any specific bike paths or cycleways in El Ejido?
A: Yes, El Ejido features dedicated cycling infrastructure. For instance, there's a notable &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4430381" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bike Path from Balerma to Guardias Viejas&lt;/a&gt;, which provides a pleasant and safe route for cyclists along the coast.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in El Ejido?
A: The touring cycling routes in El Ejido are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 230 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse terrain, from scenic coastal paths to challenging inland ascents, and the opportunity to explore natural reserves and historical sites.

Q: Can I rent touring bikes in El Ejido?
A: Yes, El Ejido makes touring cycling accessible even if you don't bring your own equipment. Well-maintained mountain bikes, road bikes, and e-bikes are available for rent, allowing you to choose the best option for your preferred route and fitness level.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by lakes or wetlands?
A: Yes, several routes in the El Ejido area offer views of lakes and wetlands, providing unique natural scenery. You can find routes that pass by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1383204" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salinas and Wetlands of Roquetas de Mar&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4103980" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Balsa de la Chanata&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5405438" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Salinas Lagoon, Roquetas de Mar&lt;/a&gt;, among others.
tips: The Castillo de Guardias Viejas is located in the town of Los Baños de Guardias Viejas, within the municipality of El Ejido, in Almería. It is a coastal battery erected in the 18th century, on a low rocky surface, close to the Mediterranean Sea.

El Ejido is a city belonging to the Poniente Almeriense region. Its name comes from the Latin word "exitum" which means "exit to the field".
Currently, it is recognized for its large area of greenhouses, known as La Huerta de Europa and El Mar de Plástico.
